top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 85, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
86ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Bali's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before February.
GFNY Bali's paved volcanic mountain roads with sustained forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 85 miles.
At GFNY Bali, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Bali. Train it until it feels boring.
7,316ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 85 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
7,316ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
The aerobic base that lets you ride paved volcanic mountain roads with sustained at tempo for 85 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 85 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At GFNY Bali, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.heat: February in Lebih, Klungkung, Bali, Indonesia can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before GFNY Bali. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for GFNY Bali’s 85 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at GFNY Bali. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at GFNY Bali requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on paved volcanic mountain roads with sustained climbing to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 85 miles of paved volcanic mountain roads with sustained climbing in Lebih, Klungkung, Bali, Indonesia.
